frameworks.name: structural alignment / structure mapping
frameworks.one_line: value representations align with neural codes for an abstract magnitude dimension

frameworks.name: mental number line / magnitude representation
frameworks.one_line: symbolic-number magnitude code is reused to encode bandit reward probability

frameworks.name: reward-guided learning
frameworks.one_line: task frame in which payout probabilities are learned for novel visual bandits

frameworks.name: neural network simulation
frameworks.one_line: connectionist model showing structural alignment can support transfer learning

core_question: Does the brain represent learned reward probabilities by aligning them with an abstract magnitude code?
key_assumptions: magnitude is an abstract relational concept compactly encoded on a single dimension
neural patterns for symbolic numbers index a stable magnitude representation
reward probabilities can be ordered on the same magnitude dimension
manipulations: viewing symbolic numbers to map magnitude code
reward-guided learning with novel complex visual bandits
varying bandit payout probability across the magnitude range
key_findings: neural patterns for novel bandits reflected their payout probability
bad bandits shared neural representation with small numbers
good bandits shared neural representation with large numbers
network simulations reproduced the structural-alignment pattern
interpretations: reward-probability learning is accompanied by structural alignment with an abstract magnitude code
such structural alignment can promote transfer learning across domains
